description: Money stored as DECIMAL(19,2) is summed for display KPIs by re-parsing each string to a JS number (IEEE-754 double) and adding in JS, instead of summing in SQL as the rest of the DAL does. Individual DECIMAL(19,2) values up to ~9e15 are exactly representable, but accumulating many of them in a double introduces representation/rounding error, so the displayed rollup can drift from the exact ledger figure by fractions of a cent up to cents once enough rows contribute. This is a website/API-consistency issue (charter item 8): the viewer's totals can disagree with the ledger's exact sums and, within a single view, with SQL-computed siblings. It is display-only — the ledger is never written and per-row values are shown exactly — hence minor, not a currency-integrity defect. The pattern repeats across the whole viewer; every KPI/rollup that sums money uses parseFloat+float addition rather than a DECIMAL sum.
recommendation: Sum money in SQL (as done for businessWealth/getTotalSupply/getMoneyFlow) and pass the string total to fmtAmtFull, or sum the minor-unit integers (multiply cents) in JS and divide only at format time. At minimum make each view's windowed total use the same SQL-exact path as its all-time sibling so the two figures can't disagree. Never fold DECIMAL money through parseFloat before summing.

occurrences.snippet: const windowVolume = volume.reduce((s, v) => s + parseFloat(v.total_volume), 0);
const totalAmount = edges.reduce((s, e) => s + parseFloat(e.amount), 0);

const volSeries = volume.map((v) => parseFloat(v.total_volume));
  const windowVolume = volSeries.reduce((s, x) => s + x, 0);
const totalVolume = vol.reduce((s, v) => s + parseFloat(v.total_volume), 0);
const totalBalance = accounts.reduce((s, a) => s + parseFloat(a.balance), 0);
const totalFineAmount = fines.reduce((s, f) => s + (f.revoked ? 0 : parseFloat(f.amount)), 0);
const total = swept.reduce((s, a) => s + Number(a.sweptAmount), 0);

verification_note: Snippet matches verbatim at PlayerDashboard.tsx:33-36. Money is DECIMAL(19,2) (V1__initial.sql: account_balances_mat.balance, ledger_postings.amount all DECIMAL(19,2)), and the DAL (lib/sql/me.ts) returns balance/credits/debits as strings. The component rolls them up cross-row with parseFloat + JS float addition (totalBalance over accounts; income/spend over trajectory days) rather than a SQL DECIMAL SUM, so accumulated IEEE-754 error can drift the displayed rollup from the exact ledger figure. It is genuinely display-only: this is the read-only economy-explorer, values are only piped to fmtAmtFull for rendering, the ledger is never written here, and per-row values are shown exactly (getPlayerTrajectory already does per-day SQL SUMs). Severity minor is fair — a website/ledger-consistency nit bounded to sub-cent/cent rounding on rollups, not a currency-integrity defect; the finding explicitly self-limits to that.
